zh.East Markham, Nottinghamshire, England: 英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾易亞斯特 馬卡姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Roger de East Markham於1070年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾時，他的父親Claron de Marcham30歲，母親Albreda de West Marcham18歲。他和Elgiva de Ulchel至少有7個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1135年在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾易亞斯特 馬卡姆享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: East
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who came from the east or who lived on the eastern side of a settlement. Compare   Estes  .
Americanized form (translation into English) of North German Ost and Swedish Öst.

variants.name: Estes
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English est ‘east’, denoting someone ‘from the east’ or someone living in the eastern part of a village or settlement. Today the name is still pronounced as two syllables, as it was in medieval times.

History: This name was brought to New England by Matthew (1645–1723) and Richard (born 1647) Estes, sons of Robert and Dorothy Estes of Dover, England. Probably unconnected is the founder of the VA and TN family of this name, Benjamin Estes (born 1736 in VA; died 1811 in TN).

nameMeanings.name: Markham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Markham Clinton or East Markham (Nottinghamshire). The placename derives from Old English mearc ‘mark, boundary’ + hām ‘village, homestead’ or hamm ‘water meadow’.

Irish: English surname used as an equivalent of Gaelic Ó Marcacháin ‘descendant of Marcachán’, a diminutive of Marcach ‘horseman, rider’ (see Markey ). This was originally a Galway surname but is now found mainly in Clare, sometimes translated into English as Ryder .

variants.name: Markey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Donal, Donovan, Fergal.
Irish (Monaghan): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Marcaigh ‘descendant of Marcach’, a name meaning ‘horseman, knight’, from marc ‘horse’. Compare Markham 2.
English: apparently from a placename meaning ‘boundary river’ in Old or Middle English, but no such place has been identified.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Roger de East Markham was born in 1070, in Nottinghamshire, England, his father, Claron de Marcham, was 30 and his mother, Albreda de West Marcham, was 18. He had at least 7 sons and 1 daughter with Elgiva de Ulchel. He died in 1135, in East Markham, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Roger d’East Markham est né en 1070, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Claron de Marcham, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Albreda de West Marcham, avait 18 ans. Il a eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille avec Elgiva de Ulchel. Il est décédé en 1135, à East Markham, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 65 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Roger de East Markham(이)가 1070년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Claron de Marcham(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Albreda de West Marcham(은)는 18세였다. 그는 Elgiva de Ulchel(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1135년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 이스트 마카에서 65세의 나이로 사망했다.
